title: Universal conditional branching patterns — building blocks for compound logic
description: Library of six canonical compound-logic patterns for relevance and constraint expressions. Each pattern is an XPath construction idiom. The patterns are intentionally limited — using these building blocks rather than inventing novel XPath constructs keeps expressions readable, testable, and behaviorally predictable across the OpenClinica evaluator.
rationale: Form designers under pressure tend to write increasingly elaborate XPath expressions to capture business logic — and the more elaborate the expression, the more likely it breaks in ways the validator can't catch. Constraining the design vocabulary to these six idioms produces expressions that any reviewer can reason about and that the evaluator handles correctly. This convention is the de facto style guide for XPath expressions in OpenClinica.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-7P during Phase B.1c-final. Plan doc had 7P as placeholder; the rule has six sub-cases (7P.a through 7P.f). Advisory like 7O — reference idioms, not fire-on-entity rules. Sub-cases: (a) AND-CHAINED multi-level gate: `${A}='Y' and ${B}='Y'`; example three-level gate `${SEX_CF}='F' and ${TPTCALC}='Screening' and ${FSH_REQD}='Y'`. (b) OR-BRANCHED alternative triggers: `${A}='Y' or ${B}='Y'`; example AE block visible when current visit has AEs OR prior visit flagged AEs carried forward `${AEYN}='Y' or ${AEYN_CF}='Y'`. (c) CROSS-FORM via _CF field: `${FIELD_CF}='value'` (uses the 7L helper pattern). (d) DERIVED-FLAG gate on calculated value: `${CALC_FIELD}=value`; example `${BMI} < 18 or ${BMI} > 40`. (e) NEGATED: `not(selected(${FIELD},'NONE'))` or `${FLAG}!='value'`. (f) END-STATE / TERMINATION disposition branching: on the DS form, different follow-up fields appear based on termination reason — relevant=`${DSDECOD}='ADVERSE_EVENT'` for AE reference fields, relevant=`${DSDECOD}='WITHDREW_CONSENT'` for withdrawal date, relevant=`${DSDECOD}='OTHER'` for free-text. These six idioms cover the universe of compound logic expressions encountered in real OpenClinica builds; novel XPath constructions outside this set should be reviewed before use.
